hypotheses.intervention: Rewrite every regressing or flagged skill around boundaries first: a 5-line trigger, 5 explicit when-NOT-to-use cases, 3 failure modes, and an abstain/default-to-no-skill rule; remove human-context leakage like 'gather the team' or age-80 projection.
hypotheses.mechanism: Discrimination is the weakest rubric dimension at 3.38, and 12/36 measured skills regress versus placebo. If those 12 skills each recover roughly 1 extra win per 3 problems, aggregate lift is 12/108 = 11.1pp.
hypotheses.test_design: Revise the 12 regressors plus 6 misleading skills. Run a paired same-problem eval with at least 216 decisive comparisons, balanced across applicable and adversarial non-applicable prompts. Success: revised pack beats same-length placebo by >=66.5% and beats current pack by >=10pp.
hypotheses.why_it_might_fail: The apparent regressors may be mostly n=3 noise; boundary text may reduce overuse but also make the agent under-apply useful models.

hypotheses.intervention: Convert procedural skills into agent-native executable checklists: inputs, ordered steps, stop condition, answer template, calibration constraints, and 'do not fabricate missing facts/probabilities' guards; cut conceptual exposition to keep length constant.
hypotheses.mechanism: Full SKILL.md beats 2-sentence triggers for procedural skills, while conceptual skills tie/lose. Actionability is only 4.00 and 6 skills mislead agents with human-only instructions, so making procedures operational should move solver behavior more reliably.
hypotheses.test_design: For all procedural skills, compare current vs rewritten vs length-placebo on 6-8 problems/skill, targeting >=200 decisive pairwise judgments. Pre-register success as rewritten-placebo >=66.5% and rewritten-current >=8pp.
hypotheses.why_it_might_fail: The solver may already infer the procedure from the skill name, so checklist detail may add prompt bulk without changing reasoning.

hypotheses.intervention: Use a hybrid injection policy: conceptual skills become compact trigger cards with boundaries; procedural skills keep full checklists. Do not inject long conceptual essays unless the task passes an applicability gate.
hypotheses.mechanism: Trigger-vs-instruction results show full guides help procedural skills but not conceptual skills the model already knows; adversarial consensus says verbosity may be net-negative. Reducing prompt dilution on conceptual skills should recover losses without harming procedural wins.
hypotheses.test_design: Classify all 39 skills as procedural/conceptual before testing. Run current full-pack vs hybrid-pack vs placebo on >=216 paired comparisons. Analyze conceptual and procedural strata separately; aggregate success requires >=10pp lift over current.
hypotheses.why_it_might_fail: The trigger-vs-full probe covered few skills, so the procedural/conceptual split may not generalize.

hypotheses.intervention: Add a router harness that first selects zero or one skill from a trigger catalog, abstains when no skill fits, and blocks overlapping cluster collisions; only then injects the selected skill.
hypotheses.mechanism: The eval shows weak discrimination, 5 overlap clusters, and several regressors likely caused by forcing the wrong mental model. A router can convert harmful forced-use cases into neutral or useful cases.
hypotheses.test_design: Run tri-arm paired eval: current auto-inject, router+skill, and same-length neutral router placebo. Use >=240 prompts: 50% intended-use, 25% near-miss, 25% should-not-use. Success: router arm beats placebo by >=66.5% overall and improves near-miss/should-not-use cases by >=15pp.
hypotheses.why_it_might_fail: The lift may come from changing task selection or abstention semantics rather than making the skills themselves better.

hypotheses.intervention: Add one contrastive micro-example per skill: bad generic application vs good agent answer, plus a compact output scaffold. Remove equivalent-length prose to preserve length control.
hypotheses.mechanism: Fidelity is high at 4.95 but behavioral win-rate is only 56.5%, implying agents understand the concepts but do not reliably execute them. Few-shot contrastive examples should convert abstract knowledge into observable answer structure.
hypotheses.test_design: Randomly assign half of skills within each overlap/procedural stratum to example rewrites and half to boundary-only rewrites. Evaluate on >=216 decisive comparisons. Success: example rewrites beat placebo by >=66.5% and beat boundary-only by >=5pp.
hypotheses.why_it_might_fail: Examples may anchor the solver to surface form, overfit the eval style, or crowd out the actual reasoning guidance.

sample_size_view: 108 comparisons is not enough. I would require about 200 decisive paired same-problem comparisons to validate a 10pp aggregate lift with reasonable power, or roughly 6 decisive problems per 36 skills. For per-skill claims, use ~8 problems/skill only for very large ~47pp effects and ~20 problems/skill for ~30pp effects.
single_highest_ev_bet: H1: boundary-first regressor repair, because it targets the weakest measured rubric dimension and has the cleanest arithmetic path to +10pp.
